This pop up also appears at other places (for e.g. when leaving the Deck Options page without clicking Save). The issue with this pop up is that "OK" and "Cancel" don't seem to be the correct responses to a message that says "Close and lose current input". 162→fun AlertDialog.Builder.checkBoxPrompt(
163→    @StringRes stringRes: Int? = null,

175→    checkBox.text = checkBoxLabel

218→/**
219→ * @param hint The hint text to be displayed to the user
220→ * @param prefill The text to initially appear in the [EditText]
221→ * @param allowEmpty If true, [DialogInterface.BUTTON_POSITIVE] is disabled if the [EditText] is empty
222→ * @param displayKeyboard Whether to open the keyboard when the dialog appears
223→ * @param callback if [waitForPositiveButton], called when [positiveButton] is pressed, otherwise
224→ *  called whenever the text is changed
225→ * @param maxLength if set, the user may not enter more than the supplied number of digits
226→ * @param inputType see [EditText.setInputType]
227→ * @param waitForPositiveButton MaterialDialog compat: if `false` [callback] is called on input
228→ * if `true` [callback] is called when [positiveButton] is pressed
229→ */
230→fun AlertDialog.input(
231→    hint: String? = null,
232→    inputType: Int? = null,
233→    prefill: CharSequence? = null,
234→    allowEmpty: Boolean = false,
235→    maxLength: Int? = null,
236→    displayKeyboard: Boolean = false,
237→    waitForPositiveButton: Boolean = true,
238→    callback: (AlertDialog, CharSequence) -> Unit
239→): AlertDialog {
240→    // Builder.setView() may not be called before show()
241→    if (!this.isShowing) throw IllegalStateException("input() requires .show()")
242→
243→    getInputTextLayout().hint = hint
244→
245→    getInputField().apply {
246→        if (displayKeyboard) {
247→            AndroidUiUtils.setFocusAndOpenKeyboard(this, window!!)
248→        }
249→
250→        inputType?.let { this.inputType = it }
251→
252→        if (!waitForPositiveButton) {
253→            doOnTextChanged { text, _, _, _ ->
254→                callback(this@input, text ?: "")
255→            }
256→        } else {
257→            positiveButton.setOnClickListener { callback(this@input, this.text.toString()) }
258→        }
259→
260→        if (!allowEmpty) {
261→            // this is called after callback() so allowEmpty takes priority
262→            doOnTextChanged { text, _, _, _ ->
263→                if (waitForPositiveButton) {
264→                    // this is the only validation filter we apply - toggle on or off
265→                    this@input.positiveButton.isEnabled = !text.isNullOrEmpty()
266→                } else if (text.isNullOrEmpty()) {
267→                    // potentially other filters in `waitForPositiveButton`.
268→                    // WARN: this could be buggy as it does not toggle the button back on
269→                    this@input.positiveButton.isEnabled = false
270→                }
271→            }
272→        }
273→
274→        maxLength?.let { filters += InputFilter.LengthFilter(it) }
275→
276→        requestFocus()
277→        // this calls callback(this, prefill). positiveButton may be disabled if there's no prefill
278→        setText(prefill)
279→        moveCursorToEnd()
280→    }
281→    return this
282→}

27→ *         .toTranslatedString(context)

30→ *         .toTranslatedString(context)
31→ *
32→ */
33→fun interface TranslatableString {
34→    fun Context.toTranslatedString(): String
35→
36→    companion object {
37→        fun by(@StringRes stringId: Int) = TranslatableString { getString(stringId) }
38→    }
39→}

44→//   instead writing the SAM as `context(Context) fun toTranslatedString()`.
45→fun TranslatableString.toTranslatedString(context: Context) = context.toTranslatedString()

content.text:
The "discard changes" dialog in Anki-Android was improved to use more descriptive button labels and messages, aligning it with Anki Desktop and modern Android apps. 

Specifically, the "OK" and "Cancel" buttons were replaced with "Discard" and "Keep editing", and the message was updated to "Discard current input?". These strings are sourced from the backend translations via `CollectionManager.TR` when possible, ensuring consistency across the Anki ecosystem.

### Changes Made:

1.  **`DiscardChangesDialog.kt`**:
    *   Converted from a `class` with a `companion object` to a Kotlin `object`.
    *   Updated `showDialog` to accept `positiveButtonText`, `negativeButtonText`, and `message` as parameters, allowing for more flexible and descriptive dialogs.
2.  **`02-strings.xml`**:
    *   Renamed the string resource `discard_unsaved_changes` to `discard` and updated its value to "Discard" to serve as a generic "Discard" action label.
3.  **Caller Updates**:
    *   Updated all call sites to `DiscardChangesDialog.showDialog` in the following files:
        *   `NoteEditor.kt`
        *   `CardTemplateEditor.kt`
        *   `DeckOptions.kt`
        *   `EditDeckDescriptionDialog.kt`
        *   `CardTemplateBrowserAppearanceEditor.kt`
    *   Each call now passes the appropriate translated strings:
        *   Positive button: `R.string.discard` ("Discard")
        *   Negative button: `TR.addingKeepEditing()` ("Keep editing")
        *   Message: `TR.addingDiscardCurrentInput()` ("Discard current input?")

These changes ensure that when a user attempts to close a window with unsaved changes, the resulting dialog provides clear, unambiguous options.

🤖 Generated with [Firebender](https://firebender.com)

218→/**
219→ * @param hint The hint text to be displayed to the user
220→ * @param prefill The text to initially appear in the [EditText]
221→ * @param allowEmpty If true, [DialogInterface.BUTTON_POSITIVE] is disabled if the [EditText] is empty
222→ * @param displayKeyboard Whether to open the keyboard when the dialog appears
223→ * @param callback if [waitForPositiveButton], called when [positiveButton] is pressed, otherwise
224→ *  called whenever the text is changed
225→ * @param maxLength if set, the user may not enter more than the supplied number of digits
226→ * @param inputType see [EditText.setInputType]
227→ * @param waitForPositiveButton MaterialDialog compat: if `false` [callback] is called on input
228→ * if `true` [callback] is called when [positiveButton] is pressed
229→ */
230→fun AlertDialog.input(
231→    hint: String? = null,
232→    inputType: Int? = null,
233→    prefill: CharSequence? = null,
234→    allowEmpty: Boolean = false,
235→    maxLength: Int? = null,
236→    displayKeyboard: Boolean = false,
237→    waitForPositiveButton: Boolean = true,
238→    callback: (AlertDialog, CharSequence) -> Unit
239→): AlertDialog {
240→    // Builder.setView() may not be called before show()
241→    if (!this.isShowing) throw IllegalStateException("input() requires .show()")
242→
243→    getInputTextLayout().hint = hint
244→
245→    getInputField().apply {
246→        if (displayKeyboard) {
247→            AndroidUiUtils.setFocusAndOpenKeyboard(this, window!!)
248→        }
249→
250→        inputType?.let { this.inputType = it }
251→
252→        if (!waitForPositiveButton) {
253→            doOnTextChanged { text, _, _, _ ->
254→                callback(this@input, text ?: "")
255→            }
256→        } else {
257→            positiveButton.setOnClickListener { callback(this@input, this.text.toString()) }
258→        }
259→
260→        if (!allowEmpty) {
261→            // this is called after callback() so allowEmpty takes priority
262→            doOnTextChanged { text, _, _, _ ->
263→                if (waitForPositiveButton) {
264→                    // this is the only validation filter we apply - toggle on or off
265→                    this@input.positiveButton.isEnabled = !text.isNullOrEmpty()
266→                } else if (text.isNullOrEmpty()) {
267→                    // potentially other filters in `waitForPositiveButton`.
268→                    // WARN: this could be buggy as it does not toggle the button back on
269→                    this@input.positiveButton.isEnabled = false
270→                }
271→            }
272→        }
273→
274→        maxLength?.let { filters += InputFilter.LengthFilter(it) }
275→
276→        requestFocus()
277→        // this calls callback(this, prefill). positiveButton may be disabled if there's no prefill
278→        setText(prefill)
279→        moveCursorToEnd()
280→    }
281→    return this
282→}
